questions.question: Why is per capita income used to compare countries?

options.B: It shows average income of a person

questions.explanation: Per capita income helps compare the average income and living standards of people.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a component of Human Development Index (HDI)?
questions.explanation: HDI includes health, education, and income — not population size.

questions.question: Why is income alone not a sufficient measure of development?

options.A: It ignores health and education factors

questions.explanation: Development also includes health, education, equality and quality of life.

questions.question: Which example shows conflicting goals of development?

options.B: Setting up a factory causing pollution

questions.explanation: Industrial growth may conflict with environmental protection.

questions.question: Public facilities are important because they:
questions.explanation: Public facilities like hospitals and schools improve living standards.

questions.question: Which state is often ranked high in HDI in India?
questions.explanation: Kerala ranks high due to better education and health indicators.

questions.question: Sustainable development mainly focuses on:

options.B: Using resources carefully for future generations

questions.explanation: Sustainable development ensures future needs are not compromised.

questions.question: Which indicator reflects health conditions of a country?
questions.explanation: IMR shows number of infant deaths per 1000 births.

questions.question: What does literacy rate measure?
questions.explanation: Literacy rate indicates level of education.

questions.question: Why do people have different development goals?
questions.explanation: Development goals vary based on needs and aspirations.
